message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  The effective reproduction number,
                  <jats:italic>
                    R
                    <jats:sub>eff</jats:sub>
                  </jats:italic>
                  , is the average number of secondary cases infected by a primary case, a key measure of the transmission potential for a disease. Compared to many countries, New Zealand has had relatively few COVID-19 cases, many of which were caused by infections acquired overseas. This makes it difficult to use standard methods to estimate
                  <jats:italic>
                    R
                    <jats:sub>eff</jats:sub>
                  </jats:italic>
                  . In this work, we use a stochastic model to simulate COVID-19 spread in New Zealand and report the values of
                  <jats:italic>
                    R
                    <jats:sub>eff</jats:sub>
                  </jats:italic>
                  from simulations that gave best fit to case data. We estimate that New Zealand had an effective reproduction number
                  <jats:italic>
                    R
                    <jats:sub>eff</jats:sub>
                  </jats:italic>
                  = 1.8 for COVID-19 transmission prior to moving into Alert Level 4 on March 25 2020 and that after moving into Alert level 4 this was reduced to
                  <jats:italic>
                    R
                    <jats:sub>eff</jats:sub>
                  </jats:italic>
                  = 0.35. Our estimate
                  <jats:italic>
                    R
                    <jats:sub>eff</jats:sub>
                  </jats:italic>
                  = 1.8 for reproduction number before Alert Level 4, is relatively low compared to other countries. This could be due, in part, to measures put in place in early-to mid-March, including: the cancellation of mass gatherings, the isolation of international arrivals, and employees being encouraged to work from home.
                </jats:p>
title: Effective reproduction number for COVID-19 in Aotearoa New Zealand

reference.unstructured: Anderson SC , Edwards AM , Yerlanov M , Mulberry N , Stockdale J , Iyaniwura SA , Falcao RC , Otterstatter MC , Irvine MA , Janjua NZ , Coombs D , Colijn C (22 April 2020).
